commands.review_summary:
The PR threads the embedded run’s `onToolOutcome` observer into tool-result finalization and adds success/error regressions for missing pending terminal-presentation entries.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +88. Total +104 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection, but not by a live Telegram or Discord run here. Current main can skip notifying the observer when the embedded result finalizer misses the pending terminal-presentation entry.

target.title: fix(groups): use 'channel' wording in intro for ChatType channel (fixes #95645) (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

For any non-DM Mattermost conversation — including a public channel — the system-prompt intro declares *"You are in a `<provider>` group chat."* even when `ChatType` is `"channel"`. This contradicts the authoritative inbound-meta block which reports `chat_type: "channel"`, causing agents to misidentify public channels as group chats and emit the wrong `chat_type` in tool arguments.

## What does this PR do?

Uses the actual `ChatType` value to select `"channel"` or `"group chat"` wording throughout the full `buildGroupChatContext` prompt — not just the intro sentence, but also destination references, participant labels, task labels, and visible-response guidance.

## Related Issue

Fixes #95645

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking)
- [x] AI-assisted (Hermes Agent)

## Changes Made

- `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts`: Added `chatTypeLabel`, `destinationLabel`, `participantLabel`, `sameDestinationLabel`, and `taskLabel` variables derived from `ChatType`. All group-chat-specific wording in the prompt now uses these labels, so `ChatType: "channel"` renders "channel" throughout.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts`: Added comprehensive test verifying correct wording for both `ChatType: "channel"` and `ChatType: "group"` across all prompt sections, including `message_tool_only` mode.

## How to Test

1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts` — all 7 tests pass
2. `grep -n 'chatTypeLabel\|destinationLabel\|participantLabel' src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts` — confirms label variables at lines 242–246

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** The `buildGroupChatContext` prompt unconditionally used "group chat" wording for all non-DM conversations, including public channels where `ChatType` is `"channel"`. This affected the intro line, destination references, participant labels, task labels, and visible-response guidance throughout the entire prompt.
- **Environment tested:** macOS 26.4.1, Node 22, OpenClaw branch `fix/mattermost-channel-intro-wording` (commit `472a5f44`)
- **Steps run after the patch:** (1) Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts` — 7/7 pass. (2) Called `buildGroupChatContext` from production source for all three modes: channel, group, and channel+message_tool_only.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts
   ✓  auto-reply  src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts (7 tests) 36ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  7 passed (7)

  $ node --import tsx -e "
  const groups = require('./src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts');
  console.log('=== ChatType: channel (full prompt) ===');
  console.log(groups.buildGroupChatContext({ sessionCtx: { ChatType: 'channel', Provider: 'mattermost' }, silentReplyPolicy: 'allow', silentToken: 'NO_REPLY' }));
  console.log('');
  console.log('=== ChatType: group (full prompt) ===');
  console.log(groups.buildGroupChatContext({ sessionCtx: { ChatType: 'group', Provider: 'mattermost' }, silentReplyPolicy: 'allow', silentToken: 'NO_REPLY' }));
  console.log('');
  console.log('=== ChatType: channel, message_tool_only ===');
  console.log(groups.buildGroupChatContext({ sessionCtx: { ChatType: 'channel', Provider: 'mattermost' }, sourceReplyDeliveryMode: 'message_tool_only' }));
  "
  === ChatType: channel (full prompt) ===
  You are in a Mattermost channel. Your text replies are automatically sent to this channel. For ordinary text, do not use the message tool to send to this same channel; just reply normally. Use message(action=send) only when you need to send files, images, or other attachments to this same channel/topic. Be a good channel participant: mostly lurk and follow the conversation; reply only when directly addressed or you can add clear value. ... When subagent or session-spawn tools are available and a directly requested channel task will require several tool calls, prefer delegating bounded side investigations early so the channel gets a responsive path forward. Keep the critical path local, avoid subagents for simple one-step work, and only surface concise channel-visible updates when they add value. ...

  === ChatType: group (full prompt) ===
  You are in a Mattermost group chat. Your text replies are automatically sent to this group chat. For ordinary text, do not use the message tool to send to this same group; just reply normally. ... Be a good group participant: ... a directly requested group-chat task ... group-visible updates ...

  === ChatType: channel, message_tool_only ===
  You are in a Mattermost channel. Normal final replies are private and are not automatically sent to this channel. ... the target defaults to this channel. Be a good channel participant: ... a directly requested channel task ... channel-visible updates ... If no visible channel response is needed ... not be posted to the channel.
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** All three modes produce correct wording. The channel prompt contains zero instances of "group chat" or "group" (except "the channel gets a responsive path" which is correct). The group prompt contains zero instances of "channel". The `message_tool_only` channel mode also uses "channel" throughout. 7/7 vitest assertions pass covering all wording variants.
- **What was not tested:** Live Mattermost channel interaction — the change is string interpolation in a pure function exercised through the production source path; no live platform needed.
